title: [REWRITE] Terrain enum expansion to 14 types
acceptance: scripts/weather/weather_type.gd defines all 14 Terrain enum values per GAME_DESIGN.md §6
Helper functions: is_walkable(), is_conductive(), is_death_tile() implemented and unit-tested
All references to old 6-value enum removed from codebase
test/test_weather_cards.gd passes after update

title: [REWRITE] WeatherCard Resource and canonical card enum
acceptance: scripts/weather/weather_card.gd extends Resource with type, display_name, description, icon, cost, radius, is_directional
WeatherType.Card enum exported from weather_type.gd matches the 6 cards in GAME_DESIGN.md §5
Resources for each card created at resources/cards/card_rain.tres through card_fog.tres
Inspector loading verified in Godot editor

title: [REWRITE] WeatherSystem pure-function interaction matrix (37 active transitions)
acceptance: scripts/weather/weather_system.gd implements WeatherSystem.apply(grid, w, h, card_type, pos) as a pure function returning a new grid
All 37 active transitions from GAME_DESIGN.md §5 matrix implemented and tested
Function never mutates input grid - verified with shared-reference test
Unit tests cover all 37 transitions plus 47 (none) cells in test/test_weather_cards.gd

title: [REWRITE] Lightning flood-fill chain through conductive tiles
acceptance: Lightning targeting any conductive tile (WATER, WET_GRASS, ICE, MUD) flood-fills connected conductive region
All tiles in the connected region become SCORCHED in a single apply() call
Lightning on ICE shatters single tile to EMPTY (no chain)
Test cases cover: single conductive tile, large connected region, ice shatter, non-conductive target

title: [REWRITE] Wind 3-tile cross effect for fog/steam dispersal
acceptance: WIND card affects target tile + 4 cardinal neighbors (3-tile cross, max 5 tiles)
Each tile in the cross independently checks WIND row of interaction matrix
Out-of-bounds neighbors silently skipped
Test cases cover: center placement, edge placement, corner placement, mixed terrain in cross

title: [REWRITE] Fog 3x3 area effect with START/GOAL/STONE exclusion
acceptance: FOG card covers a 3x3 area centered on target with FOG_COVERED
STONE, START, and GOAL tiles inside the 3x3 area are excluded (not covered)
Out-of-bounds positions silently skipped
Test cases cover: clear 3x3, mixed area with STONE, START/GOAL preservation, edge clipping

title: [CORE] GridManager v2 with queue API + undo/redo stack
acceptance: scripts/grid/grid_manager.gd refactored to support queue_card(), unqueue_last(), clear_queue(), get_queue()
Undo and redo stacks track grid mutations from sequence playback
Signals: grid_changed, card_queued, card_unqueued, sequence_started, sequence_finished
Pure logic separated from rendering - GridManager has no TileMapLayer dependencies

title: [CORE] LevelData Resource (v2 schema with start/goal/cards/par)
acceptance: scripts/level/level_data.gd implements full v2 schema per GAME_DESIGN.md §7

All fields exported: id, world, level_number, display_name, hint_text, width, height, initial_terrain, start_position, goal_positions, available_cards, max_moves, par_moves, target_difficulty, min_solution_length, unique_solution
Resource saves and loads round-trip without data loss
Validation: start_position must be walkable, goal_positions reachable per solver

title: [CORE] LevelLoader JSON read/write
acceptance: scripts/level/level_loader.gd implements load_from_json(path) returning LevelData
save_to_json(level, path) writes round-trip-stable JSON
Schema validation: missing/invalid fields produce clear error messages
Test cases cover: valid level, malformed JSON, missing required fields, schema version mismatch

title: [CORE] WorldData and WorldLoader for world meta + path JSON
acceptance: scripts/level/world_data.gd Resource with id, name, mood, level_count, music_path, ambient_path
scripts/level/world_loader.gd loads worlds + level lists for each world
Per-world path JSON loaded from levels/worldN/path.json with normalized node coordinates
Test: all 6 worlds load without error

title: [CORE] EventBus autoload (level_started, sequence_started, etc.)
acceptance: scripts/autoload/event_bus.gd registered as autoload in project.godot
Signals defined: level_started, level_completed, level_failed, card_queued, card_unqueued, sequence_started, sequence_finished, character_died, no_path_forward
All signals strongly typed with parameter signatures
Documented in scripts/autoload/event_bus.gd header comment

title: [CORE] AudioManager autoload with Music/SFX/Ambient buses
acceptance: scripts/autoload/audio_manager.gd registered as autoload
Three audio buses configured: Master > Music, SFX, Ambient
play_music(track_path), play_sfx(sound_path), play_ambient(layer_path) APIs implemented
Crossfade between music tracks on world change
Volume controls bound to SaveManager settings

title: [CORE] SaveManager autoload — schema v1 with atomic writes
acceptance: scripts/autoload/save_manager.gd implements full schema from GAME_DESIGN.md §11
Atomic writes via .tmp + rename pattern
Schema version field present, migration stub for future versions
Save triggered on: level complete, settings change, app pause/focus loss
Test coverage: round-trip, atomic write failure, missing file, corrupt file, version migration

title: [CORE] GameManager state machine (BOOT/MENU/PLAYING/PAUSED/COMPLETE/FAILED)
acceptance: scripts/core/game_manager.gd state enum and signal-driven transitions
Level loading orchestration: load LevelData, init GridManager, init CharacterController, render UI
Win/lose detection routes to LevelComplete or LevelFailed UI
State transitions logged for debugging

title: [MECH] Sequence queue model — players queue cards, commit on PLAY
acceptance: Player can tap a card to select, tap a tile to add to queue with numbered ghost preview
Tapping a queued card removes it from queue
Queue is immutable once PLAY SEQUENCE is committed
Queue strip widget renders queued cards with order numbers
Tile preview overlay shows projected post-sequence state

title: [MECH] Sequence playback orchestration (card-by-card resolve with anims)
acceptance: On PLAY SEQUENCE, cards animate from queue to target tiles one at a time
Each card triggers WeatherSystem.apply() and visual effect
Inter-card pause configurable (default 100ms)
Walk phase begins 250ms after final card resolves
Speed toggle (1x/2x) functional during playback

title: [MECH] BFS PuzzleSolver with state hashing and 200k cap
acceptance: scripts/puzzle/puzzle_solver.gd implements BFS with PuzzleState hash deduplication
MAX_STATES cap of 200000 prevents runaway on bad levels
Returns SolverResult with is_solvable, solution, min_moves, states_explored, elapsed_ms
Performance: solves a 5x5 / 6-card test level in under 2 seconds on dev hardware
Test cases: trivial level, multi-step level, unsolvable level, state cap behavior

title: [MECH] PuzzleSolver goal predicate uses A* path existence
acceptance: Default goal predicate runs A* from start to goal on walkable tiles after each candidate state
Win = path exists; the solver does NOT require character to walk - just verify path
Custom goal predicates supported via Callable parameter
Test: levels with no path are correctly marked unsolvable

title: [MECH] SolverResult difficulty_score() and par_moves derivation
acceptance: scripts/puzzle/solver_result.gd implements difficulty_score() returning 1-10
Score factors: min_moves length, branching (states_explored), unique_solution flag
par_moves = solver.min_moves used by star rating system
Cached on LevelData when level is validated by tools/dev/validate_levels script

title: [MECH] A* pathfinder over walkable terrain with cardinal moves + tie-break
acceptance: scripts/character/pathfinder.gd implements pure A* with cardinal-only movement
Walkable predicate uses WeatherType.is_walkable()
Tie-breaking prefers straight-line paths over zigzag for visual clarity
Returns Array[Vector2i] of tile positions or empty array if no path
Test cases: trivial path, blocked path, multi-route, identical-cost tiebreak

title: [MECH] Character controller — idle, walk, death state machine
acceptance: scripts/character/character_controller.gd state machine: idle, surprised, walk_n/s/e/w, cheer, drown, burn, electrocute, freeze, fall
Walks supplied path tile-by-tile at 250ms per step
Direction sprite chosen based on next tile delta
Death state plays appropriate animation, holds final frame
Win state plays cheer animation, emits character_won signal

title: [MECH] Death detection — drown/burn/fall (v1 active set)
acceptance: Before each walk step, character_controller checks tile type
WATER -> drown state, EMPTY or off-grid -> fall state, SCORCHED -> burn state
On death, character_died signal emitted with cause enum
GameManager catches signal and transitions to FAILED state
v1.5 anim sets (electrocute, freeze) have stub triggers behind feature flag

title: [MECH] Sequence undo and full-queue cancel
acceptance: UNDO button removes the most recently queued card from queue
CANCEL button clears the entire queue with confirm tap
Tile preview overlay updates after each undo/cancel
Queue strip widget animates removal smoothly
Disabled when queue is empty

title: [MECH] Move counter = queue size, max enforcement
acceptance: MOVES display shows level.max_moves at start, decrements as queue fills
When queue.size == max_moves, card hand grays out (no more queueing allowed)
Undoing a card re-enables hand
PLAY SEQUENCE button disabled when queue is empty

title: [MECH] Star rating computation (clear / under max / at par)
acceptance: Star rule per GAME_DESIGN.md §7: 1=clear, 2=moves<=max-1, 3=moves<=par
Stars passed to LevelComplete screen on win
Best stars saved to SaveManager per level
New high score detected and badge shown

title: [MECH] Hint system tier 1 (insight) + tier 2 (next move from solver)
acceptance: Tier 1 insight pulled from LevelData.hint_text, shown on goal banner always
Tier 2 hint runs solver, compares to player queue, returns next divergence move
HintPopup shows the suggested card + tile to play it on
Hint is free in v1, no cost to use
Auto-show after configurable inactivity (default 60s)

title: [MECH] No Path Forward soft-lose detection and recovery
acceptance: After sequence resolves, A* checks for path from start to any goal
If no path, NoPathForward modal is shown instead of starting walk
Modal offers Undo Last and Restart options
Character does NOT play death animation - this is a soft lose, not a death

title: [LEVEL] World 1 path layout JSON (winding map of 22 nodes)
acceptance: levels/world1/path.json with 22 nodes in winding path layout
Each node has: level_number, normalized x (0-1), normalized y (0-1)
Path visually flows from bottom-left to top-right of biome bg
No nodes outside playable area
Loaded by world_select scene without error

title: [LEVEL] World 2 path layout JSON
acceptance: levels/world2/path.json with 22 nodes
Visually distinct path shape from W1
Loads without error

title: [LEVEL] World 3 path layout JSON
acceptance: levels/world3/path.json with 22 nodes
Visually distinct path shape from prior worlds
Loads without error

title: [LEVEL] World 4 path layout JSON
acceptance: levels/world4/path.json with 22 nodes
Visually distinct path shape from prior worlds
Loads without error

title: [LEVEL] World 5 path layout JSON
acceptance: levels/world5/path.json with 22 nodes
Visually distinct path shape from prior worlds
Loads without error

title: [LEVEL] World 6 path layout JSON
acceptance: levels/world6/path.json with 22 nodes
Visually distinct path shape from prior worlds
Loads without error

title: [UI] UIManager autoload with push/pop transitions
acceptance: scripts/ui/ui_manager.gd registered as autoload
push_screen(scene_path), pop_screen(), replace_screen() APIs
Screen transitions use timings from GAME_DESIGN.md §13 (push 280ms ease-out, pop 200ms ease-in)
Modal overlays use slide-up 220ms ease-out
Reduce-motion setting halves all transition durations

title: [UI] UI theme tokens (colors, fonts, sizing)
acceptance: scripts/ui/ui_theme.gd defines color constants from UI_SCREENS.md color tokens table
Font references: display, body, numbers
Sizing constants: tile size, touch target min, side margin, vertical rhythm
All UI scenes pull from this single source - no hardcoded hex

title: [UI] Splash screen scene + auto-advance
acceptance: scenes/ui/splash.tscn with wordmark and parallax cloud bg
Auto-advance to Home after 1500ms
Tap-to-skip implemented
Save system loads in background during splash

title: [UI] Home screen — Continue / Select Level / Settings + progress strip
acceptance: scenes/ui/home.tscn matches UI_SCREENS.md Screen 2
Three vertical buttons: CONTINUE (primary), SELECT LEVEL, SETTINGS
Progress strip shows N/132 levels and total stars from SaveManager
CONTINUE button label changes: BEGIN (new save), CONTINUE (in-progress), FREEPLAY (100% complete)
Painterly hero art at top 40%

title: [UI] World Select screen (2x3 painterly grid)
acceptance: scenes/ui/world_select.tscn with 2x3 grid of world cards per UI_SCREENS.md Screen 3
Each card shows painterly biome art, world number+name, star count
Locked worlds dimmed with padlock icon
Tap unlocked card -> push level_select
Tap locked card -> shake animation + denied sound

title: [UI] Level Select world map with winding path nodes
acceptance: scenes/ui/level_select.tscn loads world_bg_wN.png as scrollable background
22 level nodes positioned per worldN/path.json
Node states: completed (green), current (blue pulsing), locked (silhouette)
Camera centers on current level on entry
Vertical drag scrolls the map

title: [UI] Gameplay screen — header, hint banner, queue strip, grid, hand, controls
acceptance: scenes/ui/gameplay.tscn matches UI_SCREENS.md Screen 5 layout exactly
Header: back arrow, level title+insight, pause icon
Hint banner shows MOVES counter and insight text
Queue strip, grid, card hand, bottom controls all wired
Sequence playback sub-state hides queue/cancel and shows speed toggle

title: [UI] Card hand widget (tap-to-select, lift, glow)
acceptance: scripts/ui/widgets/card_view.gd implements 4 visual states: default, pressed, disabled, glow
Tap-to-select: card lifts 8px with glow
Long-press shows description tooltip
Exhausted cards (in queue) at 30% opacity

title: [UI] Queue strip widget (numbered placement, drag reorder)
acceptance: scripts/ui/widgets/queue_strip.gd shows queued cards with order numbers (1, 2, 3...)
Tap a queued card to remove
Drag to reorder within queue
Empty state shows 'tap a card, tap a tile' hint text

title: [UI] Grid view widget (TileMapLayer with state-driven tile changes)
acceptance: scripts/ui/widgets/grid_view.gd renders GridManager state via TileMapLayer
Listens to grid_changed signal and updates tiles incrementally
Tile preview overlay (semi-transparent) shows projected post-sequence state
Touch input maps screen position to tile Vector2i

title: [UI] Character view widget (sprite, animation state, position tween)
acceptance: scripts/ui/widgets/character_view.gd binds to CharacterController state
Plays appropriate animation per state (idle, walk_dir, cheer, death anims)
Tweens position smoothly during walk phase
Death animation holds final frame until level_failed handled

title: [UI] Level Complete screen with star reveal animation
acceptance: scenes/ui/level_complete.tscn matches UI_SCREENS.md Screen 6
Stars animate in one at a time (200ms apart) with sparkle particles
Title and tagline change by star count
Stat panel: moves, best, par
NEXT LEVEL button advances to next level (or NEXT WORLD on world end)

title: [UI] Level Failed modal with death-specific copy and try again
acceptance: scenes/ui/level_failed.tscn modal overlay with red border
Title varies by death cause: drown, burn, fall
TRY AGAIN restores initial board, clears queue
UNDO LAST restores board with all-but-last cards re-queued
HINT button opens HintPopup over this modal

title: [UI] No Path Forward modal
acceptance: scenes/ui/no_path.tscn modal overlay with amber border
No death animation - this is a soft lose
UNDO LAST and RESTART options
Background gameplay state preserved, returnable

title: [UI] Pause modal — resume / restart / settings / quit
acceptance: scenes/ui/pause.tscn modal with PAUSED title
RESUME closes modal, returns to exact gameplay state
RESTART LEVEL reloads level
SETTINGS pushes settings screen modally
QUIT TO WORLD MAP pops to level_select

title: [UI] Settings screen — audio sliders, visual toggles, gameplay options, reset
acceptance: scenes/ui/settings.tscn with sections: Audio, Visual, Gameplay, Account, About
Audio sliders: master, music, sfx (live update)
Visual toggles: color blind mode dropdown, reduce motion, show grid overlay
Gameplay: hint timeout dropdown, confirm sequence toggle
Reset progress button with double-confirm

title: [UI] Hint popup with optimal next move display
acceptance: scenes/ui/popups/hint_popup.tscn modal
Calls solver, identifies first divergence from player queue
Shows: card sprite, arrow, target tile coordinate
GOT IT button highlights the suggested tile briefly on the board behind

title: [UI] Confirm Sequence popup (when setting enabled)
acceptance: scenes/ui/popups/confirm_sequence.tscn modal
Only shown if settings.confirm_sequence == true
Shows queued cards in order with PLAY and CANCEL options
PLAY commits sequence, CANCEL returns to planning

title: [UI] Touch input — tap thresholds, drag detection, multi-touch ignore
acceptance: Tap detection with 16px movement threshold (drag if exceeded)
Long-press at 500ms
Multi-touch ignored except in pinch-zoom (future)
Touch target minimum 144x144 px enforced in all interactive scenes

title: [UI] Mouse + keyboard parity (click maps to tap, ESC = pause, Z = undo)
acceptance: Left click maps to tap
ESC opens pause modal
Z = undo, Shift+Z = redo, Enter = play sequence, Delete = cancel queue
Arrow keys navigate card hand selection
Space toggles play sequence

title: [QA] Test coverage for WeatherSystem interaction matrix (40+ cases)
acceptance: test/test_weather_cards.gd covers all 37 active state transitions
Plus 3+ multi-tile test cases per area-effect card (wind, fog, lightning)
Total minimum 40 test functions
All tests pass via npm run validate

title: [QA] Test coverage for PuzzleSolver (trivial / multi-step / unsolvable / cap)
acceptance: test/test_puzzle_solver.gd covers: trivial 1-move solve, multi-step 5+ move solve, unsolvable level, state cap behavior
Hash collision sanity test (different states != same hash)
Performance test: 5x5 grid with 6 cards solves under 2s
All tests pass via npm run validate

title: [QA] Test coverage for SaveManager schema + atomic writes + migrations
acceptance: test/test_save_load.gd covers: save round-trip, atomic write failure recovery, missing file load, corrupt file load, schema version migration stub
Settings persistence test
Per-level stats persistence test

title: [QA] Test coverage for A* pathfinder edge cases
acceptance: test/test_pathfinder.gd covers: trivial path, blocked path (no solution), multi-route shortest path, identical-cost tiebreak preference
Edge cases: start == goal, start unreachable, goal off-grid

title: [QA] Test coverage for sequence playback (queue → resolve → walk)
acceptance: Integration test runs full level cycle headless
Queue cards, commit, verify resolved board state, verify walk path
Test win and each loss state (drown, burn, fall)
Test no-path soft lose

title: [QA] validate_all_levels.gd walks every level JSON and runs solver
acceptance: scripts/validate_all_levels.gd discovers all levels/worldN/level_*.json files
Runs PuzzleSolver on each
Exits non-zero if any level is unsolvable
Writes report to results/level_validation.json with par_moves per level
Integrated into npm run validate pipeline

title: [QA] CI green-on-main gate: no merge if validate or tests fail
acceptance: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs gut tests + validate_all_levels on every PR
PR cannot be merged if either fails
Branch protection rule enforced for main
Status check labels visible in PR UI

title: [QA] External playtest checkpoint at 25% / 50% / 75% / 100% content
acceptance: Producer agent schedules 4 external playtest sessions over content production
Each session: 3-5 testers, video recorded, notes captured in docs/playtests/
Findings triaged into Linear within 48 hours
Difficulty curve audit performed after each session

title: [ART] Replace ART_DIRECTION.md with pixel-art + painterly key art direction
acceptance: docs/ART_DIRECTION.md fully replaced per Pass 2 spec
Pixel art rules (16x16 base, no AA, integer scale)
Painterly key art exception spelled out
32-color palette referenced from styleguide assets

title: [ART] Replace ANIMATION_DIRECTION_2D.md with character + death + weather anim spec
acceptance: docs/ANIMATION_DIRECTION_2D.md fully replaced
Character animation spec for all 12 states
Per-card weather effect timings
5 death animation specs
Reduce-motion fallback rules

title: [ART] Replace ASSET_PROMPTS_GEMINI.md with prompts for new mechanics
acceptance: docs/ASSET_PROMPTS_GEMINI.md fully replaced
Pixel art prompts for tiles, cards, character, VFX
Painterly prompts for world cards, world backgrounds, hero art, sunrise scenes
Each prompt includes style anchors and rejection criteria

title: [ART] Generate 14 terrain tile sprites at 16x16 pixel art
acceptance: All 14 terrain types per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §1 produced
Animated tiles (water, scorched, steam, fog_covered, goal) have multi-frame strips
All sprites use the 32-color palette
Pixel-perfect, no anti-aliasing

title: [ART] Generate 6 weather card sprites with 4 states each (24 files)
acceptance: All 6 cards with default/pressed/disabled/glow states
Plus 6 standalone icon sprites for HUD/queue strip
Per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §2
Color-coded per UI theme (rain=cyan, sun=amber, etc.)

title: [ART] Generate Sky character — idle/walk/cheer + 5 death anims
acceptance: All 12 character animations per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §3
24x24 pixel art, hooded gender-neutral figure
Walk in all 4 cardinal directions
All 5 death animations even though only 3 ship in v1 (drown, burn, fall)

title: [ART] Generate weather VFX sprite sheets
acceptance: 12 VFX sprite sheets per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §4
Rain, sun, frost, wind, lightning bolt+chain, fog, splash, flame, sparkle, dust, confetti
All loop or one-shot per spec

title: [ART] Generate UI sprites — buttons, frames, icons, stars
acceptance: All UI sprites per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §5
4 button states (default/pressed/disabled/primary)
9-slice frames for panels
All icons at 24x24

title: [ART] Commission or generate 6 painterly world cards (240x160)
acceptance: One per world: downpour, heatwave, coldsnap, galeforce, thunderstorm, whiteout
Painterly style (NOT pixel art - the exception)
240x160 source size
Recognizable at thumbnail size

title: [ART] Commission or generate 6 painterly world backgrounds (360x800)
acceptance: Tall vertical biome paintings, one per world
Same scene as world card but extended for vertical scroll
Path through the scene must accommodate 22 level nodes
Painterly style matching world cards

title: [AUDIO] Compose 6 world music loops + menu loop + win sting (8 tracks)
acceptance: 8 OGG Vorbis tracks per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §7
Each world music ~2:30 with clean loop point
Mood matches world reference (see ASSET_MANIFEST.md table)
Mastered for mobile speaker playback

title: [AUDIO] Generate 30 SFX files with ElevenLabs SFX
acceptance: 30 WAV files per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §7 SFX list
Card placement, weather resolution, footsteps, deaths, win/lose, UI
Normalized to -3dB peak

title: [AUDIO] Record 6 ambient layers (rain, cicadas, wind, etc.)
acceptance: 6 OGG ambient loops per ASSET_MANIFEST.md §7
Designed to sit underneath music without clashing
Long enough loops (60s+) to feel non-repetitive

title: [RELEASE] GitHub Actions: Windows + Linux + Android export matrix
acceptance: .github/workflows/build.yml exports for Windows, Linux, Android in matrix
Uses barichello/godot-ci:4.6 image
Artifacts uploaded per platform
Triggered on version tags

title: [RELEASE] Steam Cloud save sync configuration
acceptance: Steam Cloud configured in Steamworks for save_default.json path
Tested across 2+ machines
Conflict resolution policy documented

title: [RELEASE] Steam page draft (capsule, screenshots, trailer placeholder)
acceptance: Steam store page draft with capsule art, header capsule, library hero
5+ screenshots from gameplay
Trailer placeholder (final trailer is separate task)
Description copy reviewed

title: [RELEASE] Privacy policy + EULA + acknowledgements page
acceptance: Privacy policy URL hosted (no PII collected -> simple template)
EULA accessible from settings
Acknowledgements page lists all OSS dependencies and licenses

title: [RELEASE] Crash reporter integration (Sentry or similar)
acceptance: Crash reporter configured for Windows/Android exports
PII scrubbing verified
Alerts route to producer agent on crash spike

acceptance: Event taxonomy defined: level_started, level_completed, level_failed, hint_used, settings_changed

Demo branch in Steamworks
